message:
mcp-rvdna(smoke): assert CORS preflight headers (iter 32 regression)

Iter 32 fixed mcp-rvdna's missing CORS layer (commit 6d900d6). The
fix has two regression checks today:
  - the cross-mcp browser smoke (verifies the wire end-to-end)
  - direct curl OPTIONS request from this commit

The browser smoke is great but it's slow (~20 s) and a chrome
install away. A direct curl preflight is ~50 ms and runs anywhere
with bash + curl, so it's the right thing to wire into the existing
http-smoke.sh — caught early with no Chrome dependency.

New `CORS preflight` section in mcp-rvdna/scripts/http-smoke.sh
asserts:
  ✓ preflight returned 204 No Content
  ✓ access-control-allow-origin: http://localhost:4173 (echoed)
  ✓ access-control-allow-methods present
  ✓ access-control-allow-headers present
  ✓ access-control-expose-headers: mcp-session-id

Total smoke is now 12 checks (build + launch + 5 CORS + handshake +
init-notify + 5 tools-list + lineage refusal); all green in 2 s wall
time. CI smoke job picks up automatically via scripts/smoke-all.sh.

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>

files.patch:
@@ -62,6 +62,32 @@ if ! kill -0 "$MCP_PID" 2>/dev/null; then
 fi
 ok "rvdna-mcp listening on ${BIND} (pid ${MCP_PID})"
 
+hdr "CORS preflight (iter 32 regression — Console at :4173 must be allowed)"
+# OPTIONS request mimicking a browser preflight from the Console.
+# Iter 32 fixed mcp-rvdna's missing CORS layer; this guards against
+# a regression that would otherwise only surface in the cross-mcp
+# browser smoke.
+PREFLIGHT=$(curl -isS -X OPTIONS "${URL}" \
+  -H 'Origin: http://localhost:4173' \
+  -H 'Access-Control-Request-Method: POST' \
+  -H 'Access-Control-Request-Headers: content-type, mcp-session-id')
+PRE_STATUS=$(echo "${PREFLIGHT}" | head -1 | awk '{print $2}')
+if [[ "${PRE_STATUS}" == "204" ]]; then
+  ok "preflight returned 204 No Content"
+else
+  err "preflight returned ${PRE_STATUS}, expected 204"
+fi
+for header in 'access-control-allow-origin: http://localhost:4173' \
+              'access-control-allow-methods:' \
+              'access-control-allow-headers:' \
+              'access-control-expose-headers: mcp-session-id'; do
+  if echo "${PREFLIGHT}" | grep -qi "^${header}"; then
+    ok "preflight carries: ${header%%:*}"
+  else
+    err "preflight missing header: ${header%%:*}"
+  fi
+done
+
 hdr "MCP handshake"
 INIT_RAW=$(curl -isS -X POST "${URL}" \
   -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
